item.title: IETLS Writing
item.content:encoded:
Part2вҖӢ

TypesвҖӢ

  • м°¬м„ұ лҳҗлҠ” л°ҳлҢҖ: Agree / Disagree
  • л‘җ к°Җм§Җ кІ¬н•ҙ: Both Views
  • мһҘм җкіј лӢЁм җ: Advantage & Disadvantage
  • мӣҗмқё / л¬ём ңм җкіј н•ҙкІ°мұ…: Cause / Problem & Solution
  • л‘җ к°Җм§Җ кіјм ң: Two-part Question

FormatвҖӢ

Agree | DisagreeвҖӢ

  • м„ңлЎ 
    • л°°кІҪ: It is true that ~
    • лӮҙ мқҳкІ¬: Although S + V, S + V. Hence, this essay agrees / disagrees ~
  • ліёлЎ 
    • л°ҳлҢҖмЈјмһҘ1: To begin with,
    • л°ҳлҢҖк·јкұ°1: That is to say,
    • мЈјмһҘ1: However, ~. First,
    • к·јкұ°1: In other words,
    • мҳҲмӢң/л¶Җм—°м„ӨлӘ…1: A study has shown that ~, A survey has shown that ~, A statistic has shown that ~, For instance,
    • мЈјмһҘ2: Moreover,
    • к·јкұ°2: That is,
    • мҳҲмӢң/л¶Җм—°м„ӨлӘ…2: In addition, In contrast, On the other hand,
  • кІ°лЎ 
    • мҡ”м•Ҫ: In conclusion,

DiscussвҖӢ

  • м„ңлЎ 
    • л°°кІҪ: There are a variety of opinions on whether S + V
    • м–‘мӘҪ мқҳкІ¬: Some people argue that S + V. However, others argue that S + V
  • ліёлЎ 
    • мЈјмһҘ1: On the one hand,
    • к·јкұ°1: That is to say,
    • мҳҲмӢң/л¶Җм—°м„ӨлӘ…1: A study has shown that ~, A survey has shown that ~, A statistic has shown that ~, For instance,
    • мЈјмһҘ2-1: On the other hand,
    • к·јкұ°2-1: In other words,
    • мҳҲмӢң/л¶Җм—°м„ӨлӘ…2-1: A study has shown that ~, A survey has shown that ~, A statistic has shown that ~, For instance,
    • мЈјмһҘ2-2: Moreover,
    • к·јкұ°2-2: That is,
  • кІ°лЎ 
    • мҡ”м•Ҫ: In conclusion,

Advantages | DisadvantagesвҖӢ

  • м„ңлЎ 
    • л°°кІҪ: It is true that ~
    • м–‘мӘҪ мқҳкІ¬: Some claim that ~ / However, others claim that ~
  • ліёлЎ 
    • л°ҳлҢҖмЈјмһҘ1: On the one hand, some argue that ~
    • л°ҳлҢҖк·јкұ°1: This is because ~
    • мҳҲмӢң/л¶Җм—°м„ӨлӘ…1: For instance, it has been reported that ~, In addition, In other ways,
    • м°¬м„ұмЈјмһҘ2-1: On the other hand,
    • м°¬м„ұк·јкұ°2-1: That is to say ~
    • мҳҲмӢң/л¶Җм—°м„ӨлӘ…2-1: As an example,
    • м°¬м„ұмЈјмһҘ2-2: Moreover,
    • м°¬м„ұк·јкұ°2-2: To be specific,
  • кІ°лЎ 
    • мҡ”м•Ҫ: In conclusion, there seem to be more drawbacks than benefits: it is ... that ...

Part1вҖӢ

FormatвҖӢ

м„ңлЎ вҖӢ

  • мҶҢк°ң
    • The graph illustrates
    • The graph provides information on
    • The table indicates various statistics on
    • The pie chars compare
  • к°ңмҡ”
    • Overall, according to the figures provided S V
    • The overall trend we can see from the graph is that S V
    • Overall, it is evident from the chart that S V
    • The significant change reflected in the statistics is that S V
    • The pattern exhibited by the charts leads to a conclusion that S V

м„ңлЎ  н‘ңнҳ„вҖӢ

  • мҰқк°Җ
    • increase / increased / increased
    • rise / rose / risen
    • there was ~ an increase, a rise
    • show / see / experience ~ an upward treand
    • reach / hit ~ a peak of / at, the top of / at
  • к°җмҶҢ
    • decrease / decreased / decreased
    • decline / declined / declined
    • fall / fell / fallen
    • drop / dropped / dropped
    • there was ~ a decrease, a decline, a fall, a drop
    • show / see / experience ~ a downward trend
    • reach / hit ~ a low of, the bottom at
  • м•Ҳм •
    • stabilise
    • remain / stay stable
    • fluctuate
  • нҳ•мҡ©мӮ¬, л¶ҖмӮ¬
    • кёүкІ©н•ң, кёүкІ©нһҲ
      • rapid / rapidly
      • dramatic / dramatically
    • мғҒлӢЁн•ң, мғҒлӢ№нһҲ
      • significant / significantly
      • considerable / considerably
    • лҜёлҜён•ң, лҜёлҜён•ҳкІҢ
      • slight / slightly
      • minimal / minimally
    • кҫёмӨҖн•ң, кҫёмӨҖнһҲ
      • steady / steadily
      • gradual / gradually

мҲҳм№ҳвҖӢ

нҚјм„јнҠён‘ңнҳ„
90%almost all
80%most of the + [UC][C]
80%vast majority of + [C]
80%four fiths
75%three quaters
70%a majority
70%slightly more than
50%a half
50%one half
45%slightly less than half
45%almost a half
45%nearly one half
30%a third
25%a quarter
25%one fourth
25%one in four
п№Ө20%less than a fifth
п№Ө20%only a small minority
п№Ө20%only, mere, merely

мҲҳлҹү н‘ңнҳ„вҖӢ

  • мҲҳ: The number of + [C]
  • м–‘: The amount of + [UC]
  • л№„мңЁ1: The percentage of
  • л№„мңЁ2: The rate of
  • л№„мңЁ3: The ratio of
  • л№„мңЁ4: The proportion of
  • мҲҳм№ҳ(лӘЁл‘җ мӮ¬мҡ© к°ҖлҠҘ): The figure for

item.title: мқёмІңкіөн•ӯмқҳ л§Өм§ҒнҢЁмҠӨ, мҠӨл§ҲнҠёнҢЁмҠӨ мӮ¬мҡ©лІ•кіј нӣ„кё°
item.content:encoded:
к°ңмҡ”вҖӢ

м¶ңкөӯмһҘм—җм„ңлҠ” мІҙнҒ¬мқё, нғ‘мҠ№кө¬, м¶ңкөӯмӢ¬мӮ¬, мҲҳн•ҳл¬јк№Ңм§Җ л„Ө лІҲмқҳ нҒ° кё°лӢӨлҰјмқҙ мһҲлӢӨ.

мқҙ мӨ‘ мІҙнҒ¬мқёмқҖ м…Җн”„мІҙнҒ¬мқёмқҙ м§ҖмӣҗлҗҳлҠ” н•ӯкіөмӮ¬л©ҙ л№ лҘҙкІҢ кұҙл„Ҳлӣё мҲҳ мһҲм§Җл§Ң лӮҳлЁём§ҖлҠ” л§ҺмқҖ кё°лӢӨлҰјмқҙ н•„мҡ”н•ҳлӢӨ. мҠӨл§ҲнҠёнҢЁмҠӨ м•ұмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳл©ҙ м¶ңкөӯмһҘ-нғ‘мҠ№кө¬мқҳ кёҙ мӨ„мқ„ кұҙл„Ҳлӣё мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ.

лӢЁкі„вҖӢ

м§‘вҖӢ

м§‘м—җм„ң м•ұмқ„ лӢӨмҡҙл°ӣкі  м—¬к¶Ңмқ„ л“ұлЎқн•ҙл‘”лӢӨ. м•„лһҳ мғҒнғңк°Җ лҗҳл©ҙ лҗңлӢӨ.

мІҙнҒ¬мқё нӣ„вҖӢ

мІҙнҒ¬мқё нӣ„ мў…мқҙ нӢ°мј“мқ„ л°ӣмңјл©ҙ нғ‘мҠ№к¶Ң л“ұлЎқ > лӮҙ мҠӨл§ҲнҠёнҢЁмҠӨ ID > мў…мқҙ нғ‘мҠ№к¶Ңмқҳ л°”мҪ”л“ң мҠӨмә”м—җм„ң л°”мҪ”л“ңлҘј мҠӨмә”н•ҳл©ҙ лҒқмқҙлӢӨ.

нғ‘мҠ№кө¬ мһ…мһҘвҖӢ

нғ‘мҠ№кө¬лЎң л“Өм–ҙк°Ҳ л•Ң л°”лӢҘм—җ ліҙл©ҙ мҠӨл§ҲнҠёнҢЁмҠӨ м „мҡ© лқјмқёмқҙ мһҲлӢӨ. кұ°кё°м—җ м•ұмқ„ мқёмӢқмӢңнӮӨкі  м–јкөҙлҸ„мһҘ м°Қкі  л“Өм–ҙк°Җл©ҙ лҗңлӢӨ. лӮҳлҠ” мӨ„мқҙ м•„мҳҲ м—Ҷм—ҲлӢӨ. мӮ¬лһҢл“Өмқҙ л§Һмқҙ кё°лӢӨлҰ¬лҠ” мӨ„ л’Өм—җ м„ңмһҲм§Җ л§җкі  нғ‘мҠ№кө¬ кІҢмқҙнҠёмӘҪмқ„ лЁјм Җ м–ҙмҠ¬л Ғкұ°л Өліҙмһҗ, м°ҫкё° нһҳл“ӨлӢӨл©ҙ кіөн•ӯм§Ғмӣҗм—җкІҢ л¬јм–ҙліҙмһҗ. мҠӨл§ҲнҠёнҢЁмҠӨлҠ” м•һмңјлЎң к°Җм„ёмҡ” лқјкі  л°”лЎң м•Ңл ӨмӨҖлӢӨ.

нӣ„кё°вҖӢ

30л¶„ мқҙмғҒ м¶ңкөӯмһҘ нғ‘мҠ№кө¬ м•һм—җм„ң кё°лӢӨл Өм•јн•ҳлҠ”кІҢ ліҙнҶөмқёлҚ°, мқҙ мӢңк°„мқ„ лҶҖмқҙкіөмӣҗмқҳ л§Өм§ҒнҢЁмҠӨмІҳлҹј л№ лҘҙкІҢ кұҙл„Ҳлӣё мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. л№ЁлҰ¬ л“Өм–ҙк°Җм„ң л©ҙм„ём җмқ„ л‘ҳлҹ¬ліј мҲҳлҸ„ мһҲкі , м•үм•„м„ң мүҙ мҲҳлҸ„ мһҲмңјлӢҲ н•ҙмҷёлЎң лӮҳк°Ҳ л•җ н•„мҲҳмқё кІғ к°ҷлӢӨ.

к№ҖнҸ¬кіөн•ӯм—җм„ңлҠ” мғқмІҙм •ліҙ мқёмҰқмқҙ мһҲлҠ”лҚ°, к·ёкұ°лҠ” кіөн•ӯм• м„ң лҜёлҰ¬ н•ҙл‘җл©ҙ лӢӨмқҢ лІҲл¶Җн„° м ңмЈјлҸ„ к°Ҳл•Ң м—„мІӯ нҺён•ҙм§„лӢӨ.

item.title: Refactoring Speaking
item.description: Overview of essential English speacking topics
item.content:encoded:
Which part of the country do you live in?вҖӢ

Seoul, capital city, crowded, 10 million people, the most safetest city

  • I live in Seoul, which is the capital city of Korea.
  • It's the most crowded city in my country.
  • In fact, about 10 million people live here.

Do you like the place where you live?вҖӢ

very convenient, close to bus stops and a subway station, many stores

  • Yes. That's because it's a very convenient place to live.
  • It's close to bus stops and a subway station.
  • There are also many stores nearby.

Have you ever purchased shoes online?вҖӢ

cheaper, check customer reviews

  • Yes, I have.
  • I like buying shoes on the internet because it's cheaper.
  • Even though I can't try them on, I can choose the right size by checking custermer reviews.

Do you often look in the mirror?вҖӢ

quite often, check myself, when trying on clothes at the shopping mall

  • Yes, I look in the mirror quite often.
  • For instance, every time I wash my hands or brush my teeth, I check myself in the restroom mirror.
  • I also use mirrors when trying on clothes at the shopping mall.

Can you play any musical instruments?вҖӢ

piano, learned to play when I was young, often play the piano to relieve stress

  • Yes, I can play the piano and I l really enjoy it.
  • I learned to play when I was a young child and often used it as a way to relieve stress

Did you ever have music classes at school?вҖӢ

music classes twice a week, learned and practiced many songs with my friends, learned about famous composers

  • Yes, I took music classes twice a week in school.
  • I learned and practiced many songs with my classmates.
  • Plus, we learned about famous composers, like Chopin and Liszt.

When was the last time you gave chocolate to someone as a present?вҖӢ

my wife about two weeks ago, bought it at a cvs, tasted really good

  • The last time I gave chocolate to my wife about two weeks ago.
  • I bought it at a convenience store as a gift for my wife
  • The chocolate tasted really good. so my wife thanked me.

What kind of fruits do you like?вҖӢ

strawberries, sweet and delicious, it has lots of vitamins

  • My favorite fruit is strawberries.
  • I love them because they are sweet and delicious.
  • Additionally, they are convenient to eat since I don't need a knife to peel them.
  • That's why I often have strawberries for dessert.

Do yo prefer funny advertisements or serious ones?вҖӢ

catchy songs, grab my attention with fancy visual and sound effects

  • Personally, I prefer funny advertisements.
  • I like them because they grab my attention with fancy visual and sound effects.
  • They have magnificent scenes with catchy songs that linger in my mind.
  • So I like funny advertisements more.

Which do you like better, writing by hand or typing?вҖӢ

much faster than writing by hand, easier to make changes when I type documents

  • I like typing more than handwriting.
  • This is because using a computer is much faster than writing by hand.
  • Moreover, it's easier to make changes when I type documents on the computer.

Do you prefer taking the subway or the bus?вҖӢ

can avoid traffic jams on the road, cheaper than taking the bus

  • I prefer taking the subway.
  • That's because I can avoid traffic jams on the road.
  • Plus, I often get motion sickness when I take the bus.

Do you use public transportation when you commute to work or go to school?вҖӢ

takes 15 minutes by subway, cheaper and more convenient

  • I use public transportation almost every single day when I go to work.
  • Actually, it only takes 15 minutes by subway to get to the office.
  • Also, taking public transportation is cheaper and more convenient than driving a car.

Are you a student or do you work?вҖӢ

always busy because of my heavy workload

  • I work.
  • I'm a software developer at a software company.
  • Actaully, I'm always busy because of my heavy workload.

In what field would you like to work in the future?вҖӢ

My goal is to start a small software company

  • I'd like to work in the field of software development.
